discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tone with small-caps (IWM -1.58%) and energy/materials weak amid Iran-war oil disruption headlines. Heavy earnings-driven dispersion in mid-caps with several 25%+ moves both directions; HVAC/building products bifurcating sharply (AAON +31% vs IBP -28%). Trump tariff threat to EU adds late-day overhang.

notable_index_moves: IWM -1.58% materially worse than SPY -0.31%, classic small-cap risk-off
XBI -2.92% and XLB -1.91% leading downside; XLE -1.81% despite oil supply fears suggests demand-destruction concern
SMH -1.76% with AVGO -3% but NVDA +1.76% — semis bifurcating
VIX only 17.11 (-1.6%) — no panic, just orderly de-risking

discoveries.name: AAON, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with multi-item 8-K including 8.01 'other events'
discoveries.thesis: A 31% single-day move on earnings + 8.01 'other events' often signals more than just a beat — possibilities include a major data-center/AI cooling contract win, raised guide, or strategic announcement bundled with the print. AAON's exposure to data-center liquid cooling makes a contract-win narrative highly plausible given the AI capex tape.
discoveries.what_confirms: Press release or 8.01 exhibit reveals a named hyperscaler contract or a meaningfully raised full-year guide on data-center backlog; sympathy buying in peers like MOD, COMF persists.
discoveries.what_kills: 8.01 turns out to be routine (dividend, buyback) and the move is purely a short squeeze on a guide-raise — in that case 31% is hard to sustain without follow-through fundamentals.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Multi-item 8-Ks containing both 2.02 and 8.01 on a single day often bundle a beat with a strategic disclosure; the 8.01 is the asymmetric piece worth reading first.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K combines 2.02 results with 7.01 Reg FD and 8.01 other events — bundling pattern consistent with strategic announcement alongside earnings.
research_pointers: Read the 8.01 exhibit for any named data-center customer or capacity expansion
Compare guide raise vs sell-side consensus magnitude — does it justify 30%+?
Check liquid-cooling peer reaction (MOD, COMF, VRT) for sympathy confirmation

discoveries.name: Installed Building Products
discoveries.setup: Housing-exposed earnings miss with peer SGI also down 10% same day
discoveries.thesis: IBP is a pure-play single-family construction installer — a 28% gap on Q1 likely reflects a real volume/margin miss compounded by housing-cycle deterioration, but magnitude on a profitable cash-generative installer feels stretched. Sympathy with WHR -12%, SGI -10% suggests a broader housing/durables read-through.
discoveries.what_confirms: Management commentary cites multi-quarter volume decline or pricing pressure; sell-side cuts estimates 15%+; new home sales data deteriorates.
discoveries.what_kills: Miss is one-time (weather, project timing) with reaffirmed full-year guide — then move is an overreaction in a name with strong FCF and buybacks.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Housing-installer mid-caps trade as high-beta proxies for the cycle; first-day 25%+ gaps often overshoot but rarely fully retrace within weeks because sell-side estimate cuts lag.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K with 2.02 + 7.01 Reg FD on same day; coincides with WHR/SGI weakness, consistent with housing-cycle miss.
research_pointers: Read 7.01 Reg FD exhibit — likely contains updated full-year outlook
Cross-check with TopBuild (BLD) reaction for direct peer confirmation
Examine organic volume vs price split in Q1 release

discoveries.name: Planet Fitness
discoveries.setup: Guide cut + canceled price hike (consumer demand signal)
discoveries.thesis: Slashing guidance AND canceling a planned price increase is a double-negative: it confirms both demand softness and management's loss of confidence in pricing power. That combination justifies severe re-rating in a unit-growth franchise model where pricing was a key thesis component. 31% is harsh but mechanism is clearly identified.
discoveries.what_confirms: May/June member churn data, peer fitness operators report similar weakness, low-end consumer commentary from Walmart/Dollar Tree.
discoveries.what_kills: Management frames cancelation as goodwill/timing rather than demand; member counts actually growing — would suggest overreaction.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a growth company cancels a previously-announced price increase, the market correctly treats it as a structural pricing-power concession, not a tactical pause. The combo with a guide cut is almost always justified-magnitude bearish.
discoveries.catalyst: Q1 earnings + guide cut + canceled price hike
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: CNBC headline confirms 'slashed guidance, canceled planned price hikes' — both bearish signals confirmed by 8-K filing.
research_pointers: Compare to low-end consumer signal in restaurants (BROS -9%, SHAK -30%) for cohort thesis
Examine same-club sales trend in release vs prior quarters
Check franchisee royalty pool implications — multi-year drag if pricing reset

discoveries.name: H&R Block
discoveries.setup: Post-tax-season earnings beat in counter-cyclical name
discoveries.thesis: HRB's fiscal Q3 captures the bulk of tax-season revenue. A 24% gap typically requires both a beat and a guide raise or capital-return announcement. Counter-trend to broader consumer cyclical weakness suggests company-specific positive catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: Material buyback authorization, dividend hike, or DIY market share recapture commentary.
discoveries.what_kills: Beat is purely a one-time tax-prep volume spike with no margin/buyback follow-through.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K 2.02 dated May 6; magnitude of move suggests beat + capital return action.
research_pointers: Look for buyback authorization size relative to market cap
Compare DIY vs assisted growth rates to gauge competitive position vs Intuit
Check FY guide for next year

discoveries.name: Coca-Cola Consolidated
discoveries.setup: Bottler earnings miss in defensive name with low float
discoveries.thesis: COKE has rallied massively over recent years on margin expansion thesis; a 16% gap on a bottler is unusual and likely reflects margin compression (PET, aluminum, freight) plus volume softness. Low-float dynamics can amplify; possible overshoot but bottler margin reversals tend to persist multiple quarters.
discoveries.what_confirms: Margin guidance cut, volume declines persistent, peer Reyes/Liberty bottler commentary similar.
discoveries.what_kills: One-time hurricane/weather impact with margin recovery in Q2 commentary.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Low-float defensive names with extended multiples can deliver outsized first-day reactions to margin disappointment because the entire thesis was multiple-driven.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Q1 8-K filed May 6; magnitude of move on a bottler implies meaningful operating miss.
research_pointers: Compare gross margin vs prior 4 quarters for trend break
KO parent commentary for read-through on US bottler segment
Insider/Coca-Cola Co. ownership dynamics — limits float, amplifies moves

discoveries.name: Whirlpool
discoveries.setup: Cyclical durables miss converging with housing weakness
discoveries.thesis: WHR is heavily levered to housing turnover and consumer durables; with IBP -28% and SGI -10% same day, this is a broader housing-cycle theme rather than an isolated event. Whirlpool also carries dividend-cut risk given high leverage and weak FCF — 12% drop is consistent with that overhang.
discoveries.what_confirms: Dividend cut announced or signaled, full-year guide reduced, peer ELUX/SGI commentary aligns.
discoveries.what_kills: Tariff-relief commentary or cost actions offsetting volume — though current Trump EU tariff post argues against this.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Earnings 8-K; move clusters with other housing-exposed names same day.
research_pointers: Dividend coverage ratio post-Q1
Tariff exposure given Trump EU tariff threat today
North America margin trend

discoveries.name: Lumen Technologies
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings drift with second 8-K filing (8.01) two days later
discoveries.thesis: Lumen reported May 5; today's additional 8.01 'other events' filing combined with continued slide suggests follow-on disclosure (debt action, customer loss, AI-connectivity contract update). Volume only 1.29x average is notable — selling pressure isn't massive, suggesting drift rather than capitulation.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8.01 filing reveals debt restructuring step, customer concentration warning, or AI-contract delay.
discoveries.what_kills: 8.01 is benign (annual meeting, routine matter) — then move is sympathy with telco/leveraged names.
discoveries.what_to_learn: A second 8-K filed within 48 hours of an earnings 8-K is rarely benign; read it before drawing conclusions about post-earnings drift.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Two 8-Ks within 48 hours — second one (8.01 only) is the asymmetric signal worth reading.
research_pointers: Read May 7 8-K exhibit — what specifically was disclosed?
Bond market reaction in LUMN debt as confirmation
AI-fiber contract pipeline commentary on call

discoveries.name: Paycom Software
discoveries.setup: Earnings beat in beaten-down HCM software name
discoveries.thesis: PAYC has been a heavily-shorted disappointer over the last two years. A 10% rally on earnings with moderate volume in a risk-off tape is a healthy beat — possibly with stabilization in Beti automation cannibalization narrative that has plagued the stock. Counter-tape strength is meaningful.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reaffirmed/raised guide, sales productivity metrics improving, ADP/PCTY commentary corroborates HCM stabilization.
discoveries.what_kills: Beat was driven by float income or one-time items rather than core SaaS growth.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K 2.02 May 6; rally against weak small-cap tape suggests genuine beat.
research_pointers: Net new ARR / customer adds vs consensus
Operating margin trajectory vs prior guide
Read-through to PCTY, DAY, ADP

discoveries.name: DXP Enterprises
discoveries.setup: Sharp drop with no 8-K, low relative volume
discoveries.thesis: Empty catalyst_signals and only 1.65x volume on a 17% drop is unusual — could be a delayed earnings reaction, a private placement leak, or an industrial-distribution sympathy move (with XLI -1.6% and oil/energy tape weak, DXPE's energy-services exposure may be the driver). Without a filing or news, call this honestly: don't know.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K filed in next 24-48 hours; oil-services peers (NOW, MRC) trade weak in sympathy.
discoveries.what_kills: Stock recovers on no news — confirms it was thin-trading dislocation.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a 17% move shows up with sub-2x volume and no filings, the absence of evidence is itself the signal: probably illiquid dislocation or delayed catalyst, not a tradeable thesis yet.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} — no SEC filing, no earnings; energy-tape weakness is plausible but unconfirmed.
research_pointers: Monitor SEC EDGAR for late filing
Check for sell-side downgrade today
Energy-services peer action (MRC Global, NOW Inc)

discoveries.name: Middleby Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings + prior 7.01 Reg FD three days earlier (sequenced disclosure)
discoveries.thesis: MIDD filed a 7.01 Reg FD on May 4 — three days before the earnings 8-K — which often precedes strategic announcements (spin-off update, segment sale). Combined with the 11% rally, possible the company advanced a previously-discussed Food Processing or Residential spin-off plan.
discoveries.what_confirms: Spin-off transaction structure or timeline announced; CEO commentary on portfolio simplification.
discoveries.what_kills: May 4 8-K turns out to be investor day deck only, with no spin commitment.
discoveries.what_to_learn: A Reg FD 8-K filed in the days BEFORE earnings often telegraphs strategic content in the print itself; sequence the readings.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Two 8-Ks in 4 days with 7.01 disclosure preceding 2.02 earnings — pattern consistent with strategic update.
research_pointers: Read May 4 7.01 exhibit for spin-off language
Activist (if any) holdings in MIDD
Foodservice peer reaction

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Housing/durables earnings cluster (IBP, WHR, SGI all sharply down on Q1)
AAON +31% on earnings + 8.01 (likely data-center cooling beat/contract)
PLNT and SHAK both down ~30% on consumer demand softness same day

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump threatens EU tariffs jump 'to much higher levels' if EU doesn't cut tariffs to zero by July 4, 2026 (US 250th birthday)
trump_signals.note: Hard deadline (~2 months) creates tariff overhang for EU-exposed importers and US auto/durables competing with EU imports. WHR's drop today could be partly tariff-input cost concern. Watch for reciprocal moves; deadline structure suggests negotiation rather than imminent action.

ai_announcements.headline: How Anthropic's Mythos has rewritten Firefox's approach to cybersecurity
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic's Mythos tool has been used by Mozilla security researchers to find high-severity bugs in Firefox. This is an AI-driven vulnerability discovery story.
ai_announcements.note_anthropic_bias_flag: Anthropic-related call. No publicly traded cybersecurity name moved meaningfully today on this in the provided market data. The named beneficiaries/victims would be cyber vendors (CRWD, PANW, S, FTNT) but none appear in moves list. Counter-thesis: AI bug-finding INCREASES cyber spend and is supportive for cyber stocks. No actionable signal.

ai_announcements.headline: H&R Block (HRB) +23.7% — earnings, despite ongoing AI tax-prep narrative
ai_announcements.summary: HRB rallied hard post-earnings. Worth flagging because AI-driven tax prep (ChatGPT/Claude doing taxes) has been a recurring bear thesis. The move suggests AI displacement fears were overdone heading in.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Complex returns, audit support, in-person service, and regulatory trust are sticky. AI chatbots can't sign returns or take liability. HRB integrating AI itself reduces displacement risk.
research_pointers: Confirm earnings beat magnitude and FY guidance vs consensus
Check DIY filer count trend — is AI actually eroding the base?
Look at INTU (TurboTax) reaction as cross-check
Kills thesis: if move is purely buyback/cap return rather than ops

ai_announcements.headline: Paycom (PAYC) +9.5% — earnings, ongoing 'AI replaces HR software' narrative
ai_announcements.summary: PAYC rallied on earnings. Relevant because HR/payroll SaaS has been one of the most-shorted AI-displacement categories (agents replace HRIS workflows). Bounce suggests AI fears were overcooked.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Payroll requires regulatory compliance, tax filing across jurisdictions, and integration with banking rails — agents don't replace this. PAYC is deploying its own AI (Beti).
research_pointers: Verify earnings vs estimates, retention rate, RPO growth
Check sympathy moves in PCTY, ADP, PAYX
Kills thesis: if move fades within days as AI displacement narrative reasserts

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large moves in the market data (PLNT -31%, AAON +31%, IBP -27%, COKE -15%, WHR -12%, BROS -9%, SGI -10%, FUN +15%, MIDD +11%, LFST +20%, RRX -11%, VNT -12%, NVST -10%, LGND -9%, UGI -8%, MGEE -7%, LUMN -14%, DXPE -17%) are earnings-driven and unrelated to today's AI announcements. The Perplexity Mac agent, Spotify AI audio, Moonshot funding, and Anthropic Mythos stories have no clean read-through to the moving tickers in this dataset. Quiet day on AI-driven equity dislocations.
